rankings.blurb: Six Cornerstones. SIX. Ja'Marr Chase, JSN, Drake London, Jayden Daniels, De'Von Achane, and Omarion Hampton — this roster reads like someone cheated the draft. Four Foundational pieces backing them up including Justin Herbert and Trevor Lawrence means Sven has more franchise QBs than some teams have serviceable starters. The only knock is a barren pick cupboard and some genuinely baffling recent trades where he got absolutely fleeced by nchernandez19 twice in back-to-back days. Bro, stop trading with your division rival — you're basically mailing them Christmas presents.

Drake Maye, Jahmyr Gibbs, Puka Nacua, and Trey McBride as your top four is the kind of roster that makes other owners question their life choices. Seven Cornerstones and Foundationals with elite positional grades across the board — this team is built to win NOW and still has a runway. The Gibbs acquisition technically cost a fortune in picks but the dude is a top-3 RB in the format, so the value loss is forgivable. Only one first-round pick in the vault is the one thing keeping this team from being a unanimous No. 1.

The highest total dynasty value in the league by a country mile — 155K — and jsinykin is classified as a 'Reload,' which means this team is the empire between empires. Seven first-round picks and a roster headlined by Caleb Williams, Jaxson Dart, Jeremiyah Love, and Emeka Egbuka screams future dynasty titan in the making. The WR corps at 54.6% of total value is a little top-heavy and current production is a question mark, but trading Breece Hall and late picks for two 2027 firsts shows this guy knows how to build. Give it a year and this might be the scariest team in the league.

Josh Allen at the top of any roster is a cheat code, full stop, and pairing him with Ashton Jeanty gives gavinw20 arguably the best QB-RB combo in the league. Bo Nix as the QB2 is quietly underrated, and Garrett Wilson and Zay Flowers give this team legitimate WR depth. The ceiling here is massive, but the WR room is a little thin on upside beyond Wilson, and losing a first to get Baker Mayfield back is the kind of trade that makes you do a double-take — brother, you had Tyler Shough and a first, what are you doing?

Joe Burrow, Amon-Ra St. Brown, and Jalen Hurts form a legit core that can compete today, and Tyler Warren at TE is a sneaky great piece. But this team just got absolutely torched by nchernandez19 in a trade that cost them Jameson Williams, a 2029 first, AND a 2029 second for Malik Willis and Michael Wilson — whoever approved that deal should be removed from the group chat immediately. Zero first-round picks in the vault is terrifying for a team that's not a clear-cut championship favorite, and the depth past the top three or four is mostly Mainstays and Serviceables. Window is open but the hinges are getting rusty.

Bijan Robinson and Brock Bowers are two of the most valuable dynasty assets in the game, and nchernandez19 has been an absolute shark in the trade market lately — fleecing Sven twice and robbing GiantHawkTua blind for a collection of future firsts. Six first-round picks gives this team serious upside in the rebuild pipeline, and Jordan Love at QB provides a stable bridge. The WR room is a dumpster fire outside Marvin Harrison though, and relying on C.J. Stroud and Geno Smith as QB2/3 is the kind of depth chart that gives you nightmares. This team is going places but it is NOT going there today.

Selling Ja'Marr Chase — arguably the most valuable WR in dynasty — and ending up with Ladd McConkey, two 2028 firsts, and a 2027 first is a fine return on paper, but then turning around and flipping McConkey for Jalen Coker and a third is just bewildering roster management. Malik Nabers is the lone Cornerstone holding this thing together while Fernando Mendoza and Dak Prescott battle for QB1 duties, which should tell you everything you need to know. Seven first-round picks is genuinely exciting and the future value is real, but the current player pool is so thin that this team is essentially a fantasy farm system right now. Enjoy the Skyline Chili, Seth — you've got a long offseason ahead of you. Wait, wrong owner.

Justin Jefferson and CeeDee Lamb are a dynamic duo that would make any dynasty owner drool, but everything else on this roster smells like a team trying to outrun Father Time. Christian McCaffrey and Saquon Barkley as the RB1 and RB2 is a 'win now at all costs' strategy that is currently costing this team its long-term viability. Brock Purdy, Jared Goff, and Sam Darnold at QB is the most aggressively mediocre position group in the league, and with basically no picks in the vault and a total dynasty value of just 94K, this team is stuck in purgatory. The 'Window Contender' tag is generous — the window is cracked open but the frame is rotting.

Lamar Jackson AND Patrick Mahomes in the same starting lineup is the kind of flex that makes you think this team is invincible — until you look at the RB room and see Derrick Henry, Josh Jacobs, and Tony Pollard, which is basically a walking injury report from 2027. Zero picks. ZERO. The total pick value is literally listed as 0, meaning Herschey6153 has mortgaged every future draft capital to field a roster whose median player age is approximately 'veteran minimum.' The WR corps of McConkey, AJ Brown, and Tee Higgins is solid, but without draft capital and with a TE room of Goedert and aging Kelce, this team is one injury away from a crisis. Win now or you've won nothing.

Congratulations to sethfriedman12 for successfully trading away Jahmyr Gibbs — a top-3 dynasty RB — for a pile of picks and Cam Skattebo, which is the dynasty equivalent of trading a Ferrari for five Camrys. The pick haul is genuinely impressive and five first-rounders gives this team real rebuild upside, but Tyler Shough as your QB1, Aaron Rodgers as QB2, and Quinn Ewers as QB3 is a fever dream of uncertainty at the most important position in football. George Pickens is the only real player on this roster worth starting in a competitive lineup, and the positional grades across the board are bottom-third in the league. This is a full-on teardown in progress — enjoy the tank, and for the love of dynasty, please get a quarterback.
